subject: [PATCH] btrfs: fix a regression where PAGECACHE_TAG_DIRTY is never cleared

reviews.author: Qu Wenruo
reviews.summary:
Reviewer Qu Wenruo noted that truncation should wait for writeback to complete before invalidating a folio, and suggested exploring this idea without breaking existing functionality.

Reviewer Qu Wenruo expressed concern that the patch may introduce additional overhead and break cachestat(), requesting the v2 fix to be used as a hotfix first.
reviews.raw_body:
Writeback still has the folio locked, so truncation should wait for the 
writeback first then invalidate it.

The other direction would also be true, if the invalidation invalidated 
the page first, then writeback should not got a dirty folio anymore.

But I can be totally wrong considering how complex things are.

I can explore if this idea won't break the existing runs first.

---

Sounds great. Feel free to include the v2 fix:
https://lore.kernel.org/linux-btrfs/d47af8cae38e32462d61cb62139c2732e4e8fdbe.1783407237.git.wqu@suse.com/T/

Although considering this is already breaking cachestat() and will cause 
extra overhead for writeback, I'd prefer the v2 as a hot fix first.

Thanks,
Qu

2026-07-08.patch_summary: This patch addresses a regression in Btrfs where PAGECACHE_TAG_DIRTY is never cleared, leading to potential issues such as csum corruption when mmap writes are allowed on folios under writeback. The problem arises from the removal of folio_clear_dirty_for_io() and folio_mkclean(), which previously ensured that folios were write-protected during writeback. To fix this, the patch likely takes a lock in writeback to prevent races between truncate and writeback operations, ensuring that csums are correctly computed.

reviews.author: Boris Burkov (author)
reviews.summary: Author acknowledged that the swapoff path needs to drop the per-vswap spinlock before calling try_to_unmap(), agreed to restructure in v2, and plans to send a separate patch to fix csum corruption.
reviews.raw_body:
I gave your v2 my r-b, and will send the patch to fix the corruption
next, separately. It shouldn't affect your patch, as it only conflicted
if we went back to folio_clear_dirty_for_io().

2026-07-07.patch_summary: This patch addresses a regression in Btrfs where PAGECACHE_TAG_DIRTY is never cleared, leading to potential issues such as csum corruption when mmap writes are allowed on folios under writeback. The problem arises from the removal of folio_clear_dirty_for_io() and folio_mkclean(), which previously ensured that folios were write-protected during writeback. To fix this, the patch likely takes a lock in writeback to prevent races between truncate and writeback operations, ensuring that csums are correctly computed.
